title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Go Client Systems · Runtime Contract: Cookie Jar Scope
summary: A multi-tenant operator client rotates short-lived bearer tokens and isolated cookie jars without logging or forwarding credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go cookie jar scope increment in a Go 1.26 net/http least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Cookie Jar Scope · Theory Cookie Jar Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model handles host-only session cookie SID-5, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries. Competency probe: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
